title: rubus disjectus
intro: Rubus disjectus, also known as the disjunct bramble or mountain dewberry, is a type of wild raspberry species native to eastern North America. The plant's medicinal properties are attributed to its high content of anthocyanins and ellagic acid, which have been shown to possess antioxidant and anti-inflammatory properties. The health benefits of Rubus disjectus include its potential to reduce inflammation, improve cardiovascular health, and support the immune system, making it a popular ingredient in herbal remedies. Herbal preparations of Rubus disjectus can be found in various forms, including teas, tinctures, and infusions, which are often used to treat a range of health issues, from digestive problems to skin conditions.
